channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: If only your EV did this: Dell’s new supercapacitor keyboard and mouse combo delivers 'full day use' with a 5-second charge — here’s how
item.description: Dell’s supercapacitor-powered keyboard and mouse deliver ultra-fast charging and long usage, redefining convenience and eliminating reliance on traditional batteries.

item.title: Michael B. Jordan’s First Post-Oscars Film Looks Way Cuter Than ‘Sinners’
item.description: It's called 'Swapped' and features the Oscar winner as the voice of an animal that swaps bodies with its enemy.

item.title: CFTC sues three states for trying to regulate prediction markets
item.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is suing Illinois, Arizona and Connecticut for attempting to outlaw or regulate prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket. The CFTC believes it has sole jurisdiction to regulate these platforms, and that states attempting to classify them as illegal gambling are overstepping their authority.CFTC defines prediction markets as “designated contract markets” where futures contracts are traded, essentially letting people bet on the outcome of events (for example, who will be the Democratic nominee for president in 2028). And because futures contracts ...

item.title: 'Side effects may include curiosity': Google's $3 ChromeOS Flex kit aims to save your old Windows 10 laptop from the scrapheap
item.description: Google's kit aims to breathe new life into old hardware, and that could be Macs as well as Windows 10 PCs.

item.title: 'I was stuck on the overpass with dump trucks all around me': A mass Baidu robotaxi outage just caused traffic mayhem in China
item.description: Collisions, delays and traffic chaos have been reported due to a mass robotaxi outage in China.

item.title: ElevenLabs releases a new AI-powered music generation app
item.description: ElevenMusic lets users create and remix songs using text prompts

item.title: LLMs Will Protect Each Other if Threatened, Study Finds
item.description: They stick together.

item.title: PSA: YouTube will be streaming Coachella for free next week
item.description: Coachella officially kicks off next Friday, April 10. But for anyone who doesn’t want to fly out to the desert or fork over upwards of $650 for a ticket, YouTube is going to livestream the concert for free so you can enjoy it on your own terms. Here’s a quick overview of all the programming that will be available in just over a week. With YouTube planning on providing feeds of seven different stages, there will be no shortage of content. This includes the Quasar stage in both horizontal and vertical formats, so no matter what device you're using, there should be an aspect ratio that works. Fur...

item.title: Your Art Can Go in This San Francisco Alley
item.description: A trio of tech pranksters have launched a website where you can submit artwork and vote on which pieces belong in the final design. Of course, AI will scan for dick pics.

item.title: A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
item.description: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.

item.title: Five questions for the guys who made a compass that points to the Times Square Olive Garden
item.description: Here's a question almost nobody is asking: how do you get to the Times Square Olive Garden? Well, these beautiful weirdos are answering it - the team that made the Times Square Olive Garden compass. If that's not clear enough, let me explain. They're making a compass that points you exclusively in the direction of […]

item.title: NASA astronauts prove that sending an email really is rocket science
item.description: NASA Artemis II commander Reid Wiseman faced the most earthly possible conflict while in outer space: his Outlook wasn't working.

item.title: The Octopus Penis Arm Doesn’t Just Deliver Sperm—It Sniffs Out the Sweet Spot
item.description: The hectocotylus is both a reproductive and sensory organ, Harvard scientists and others have found.

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all mark our calendars for June 7th as an IPO date just in case. Based on the April […]

item.title: Here's What Can Happen When the US Bombs Iran's Nuclear Sites
item.description: As strikes continue on Iran’s nuclear facilities, the real danger isn’t the explosion, but what happens if critical safety systems fail—and how that risk could spread across the Gulf.

item.title: I ran my first marathon with the Garmin Forerunner 265s — it's 25% off right now, and I'd recommend it to almost every runner
item.description: Get 25% off the Garmin Forerunner 265s at Amazon, and 16% off the larger 265.

item.title: Test en vidéo : le Samsung Galaxy S26 vaut-il vraiment son tarif ?
item.description: Nous avons testé le Samsung Galaxy S26 pour vérifier si ce modèle justifie son prix face aux autres propositions du marché. Découvrez en vidéo pourquoi ce smartphone parie avant tout sur l'évolution de son interface au quotidien.

item.title: Revisiting the Ending of ‘Chainsaw Man’ Through the Lens of Japanese Folklore
item.description: Pochita's final act, in new light, can be reframed in Tatsuki Fujimoto's ambiguous 'Chainsaw Man' finale as something far more intentional and tender.

item.title: I'm a homes expert — here are my top buys from Walmart's big Patio & Garden Event
item.description: If you've been thinking about sprucing up your outdoor space for summer, now's the time to do it.

item.title: Test de l’iPad Air M4 : une mise à jour paresseuse qui pose la question de son identité
item.description: Apple vient de rafraîchir son iPad Air avec la puce M4 et 12 Go de RAM. Sur le fond, c'est une vraie progression technique. Sur la forme, c'est le statu quo absolu : même design, même écran 60 Hz, même Touch ID. On a passé une semaine avec cette tablette pour comprendre à qui elle s'adresse vraiment.

item.title: 'We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars' — Elon Musk mourns the officially retired Tesla Model S and Model X
item.description: Tesla officially kills off the once-pioneering Model S and Model X, as it focusses its future on autonomy.

item.title: I found the best Geekom mini PC deals in the Easter sale — save big on some of our highest rated and reviewed mini PCs if you're quick
item.description: I've found big savings on Geekom's mini PCs, discounted for Easter — but hurry, the sale ends soon.

item.title: U.S. Considers Ban on Chinese Air Bags Blamed for Deaths of 10 People in Survivable Crashes
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said, "these substandard parts are killing American families."

item.title: Apple AirPods Pro 3, Forza Horizon 6 et MacBook Air M5 : les meilleures promos du jeudi 2 avril 2026 à saisir
item.description: Ce jeudi 2 avril 2026, les bons plans ne manquent pas pour celles et ceux qui aiment la tech et les jeux vidéo.

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: OpenAI has purchased TBPN, an online talk show that often interviews AI executives and other tech leaders. The show goes live every weekday at 2PM PT, often for a three-hour duration, counting OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, as well as executives from Meta, Microsoft, Palantir, and Andreessen Horowitz, among its past guests, and Bloomberg, CNBC, and […]

item.title: Dell just made its thinnest Pro Premium executive laptop yet — a magnesium alloy featherweight that finally makes carrying a workstation feel effortless
item.description: Dell’s 14-inch Pro Premium combines a lightweight magnesium chassis, optimized motherboard, AI-capable processors, and high-quality display for executive mobility.

item.title: Verge subscribers: Join us for a movie night in New York City, hosted by The Vergecast
item.description: The Vergecast is hosting a special in-person screening of Sneakers in partnership with IFC Center in New York City, and we're opening presale tickets exclusively to Verge subscribers. Sneakers is a movie about what happens when technology becomes more powerful than our ability to control it. It's about privacy and hacking and who the "good […]

item.title: How American independent bookstores made a massive comeback
item.description: Amazon once seemed poised to wipe out the American bookstore. As online shopping exploded in the late 1990s and early 2000s, independent shops struggled to compete with endless inventory and lower prices. By 2009, many believed indie bookstores were on the brink of extinction. But instead of disappearing, they adapted. The future of books, it […] Amazon once seemed poised to wipe out the American bookstore. As online shopping exploded in the late 1990s and early 2000s, independent shops struggled to compete with endless inventory and lower prices. By 2009, many believed indie bookstores were o...

item.title: « C’est un cancer » : pourquoi ce sénateur américain veut-il à tout prix interdire les voitures chinoises aux États-Unis
item.description: Les voitures électriques chinoises sont moins chères, souvent mieux équipées et technologiquement compétitives. Les Américains commencent à s'y intéresser sérieusement. Et c'est justement là que ça coince.

item.title: Samsung’s New Frame TVs Look Even More Like Actual Paintings
item.description: Best of all, the 2026 "art" TVs come in cheaper 55-inch sizes.

item.title: Indie Pass is a forthcoming subscription service exclusively for indie games
item.description:
Publisher and game management platform indie.io just announced the pending launch of something called Indie Pass. This is a subscription service, so it's sort of like Game Pass but for indie titles. It launches on April 13 and will offer over 70 games on that date, with more coming down the line. Not a single person on this planet wants another monthly subscription to manage, but this one costs just $8. That's a pretty good deal, considering Game Pass Ultimate costs a whopping $30 per month.
However, these subscription platforms are only as good as their libraries. The company has already con...
